message:
test(readme): module-level null/dtype helpers functional coverage

8 module-level pandas-equivalent helpers were called in the lib.rs
prelude_completeness_compile_guard but only as `let _ = fn(&values)` —
their results were never asserted. A regression in any of these
algorithms would not have surfaced.

Adds readme_module_level_null_helpers — reachable via prelude alone —
that asserts on results from input [Int64(1), Null(NaN), Int64(3)]:

- isna     → [false, true, false]
- isnull   → [false, true, false] (alias of isna)
- notna    → [true, false, true]
- notnull  → [true, false, true] (alias of notna)
- count_na → 1
- fill_na with Int64(0) replacement → [Int64(1), Int64(0), Int64(3)]
- dropna   → [Int64(1), Int64(3)] (length 2)

And on mixed/homog Vec<Scalar> for type inference:
- infer_dtype([Int64, Float64])    → Float64 (widening)
- infer_dtype([Int64, Int64, Int64]) → Int64

Each path's result is matched element-wise so a regression in any
helper's algorithmic logic surfaces with the wrong value.

Closes frankenpandas-fd90.48.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
